pi_items.source: Confirmed in setup_task.sh line 63, export_result.sh lines 16-23, and task.json hook definitions

Verified via seed_demodb.py: Maria Garcia has Spanish nationality (line 196), Spain exists as country (line 183). However, setup_task.sh does NOT explicitly ensure this profile exists (no grep for 'Spanish'). More critically, verifier.py lines 62-66 only checks 3 mappings (American, British, Dutch), NOT Spanish. This mapping is listed in metadata but NOT verified by the verifier.

Verified via seed_demodb.py line 204: Carlos Lopez has Mexican nationality. Setup_task.sh lines 54-60 explicitly ensure this profile with Mexican nationality. Mexico does NOT exist in the countries list (seed_demodb.py lines 173-186), making this an orphan.
Confirmed in seed_demodb.py line 204 and setup_task.sh line 57

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the OrientDB demodb database with real-world data: profiles have nationalities (American, British, Dutch, Spanish, Mexican) and countries exist with proper names (United States, United Kingdom, Netherlands, Spain). Four nationality-to-country mappings are listed in metadata, but only three are actually verified by the verifier code (American→United States, British→United Kingdom, Dutch→Netherlands; Spanish→Spain is NOT checked). The Mexican profile (carlos.lopez@example.com) is correctly identified as an orphan since Mexico does not exist in the countries table. The report path is /home/ga/nationality_audit.txt and the required edge class is 'IsCitizenOf'.
